disease.name: Ampullary Carcinoma
disease.note: Malignancy arising at the ampulla of Vater (duodenal papilla). Presents earlier than pancreatic head cancer due to early biliary obstruction. Classically causes intermittent, 'fluctuating' jaundice (necrosis and sloughing of tumor temporarily relieves obstruction). Better prognosis than pancreatic cancer (5-year survival 40-60% after resection) due to earlier presentation and lower incidence of nodal metastasis.

severityAssessment.severityLabel: Resectable -> URGENT Whipple; Metastatic -> PALLIATIVE stent + chemo

historyFeatures.note: Classic presentation; may be intermittent/fluctuating (patency of biliary orifice varies)
Key distinguishing feature from pancreatic cancer

laboratory.name: LFTs
laboratory.indication: obstructive pattern: conjugated hyperbilirubinemia, elevated ALP, GGT

laboratory.name: CA 19-9
laboratory.indication: elevated but less than pancreatic cancer

laboratory.name: Fecal occult blood or Hb
laboratory.indication: may be positive from ampullary bleeding

imaging.name: CT abdomen (pancreatic protocol)
imaging.indication: small ampullary mass; may be subtle; dilated CBD and pancreatic duct; no pancreatic head mass

imaging.name: MRCP
imaging.indication: dilated CBD and PD (double duct sign); may visualize tumor at ampulla

imaging.name: Upper GI endoscopy (EGD) with side-viewing duodenoscope
imaging.indication: direct visualization of ampulla: protruding, ulcerated, or friable ampullary mass

imaging.name: EUS with FNA
imaging.indication: tissue diagnosis; T and N staging; assess relationship to SMV/PV

pathways.criteria: No distant metastases, no vascular invasion, good PS
pathways.treatment: Pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure). Prognosis significantly better than pancreatic head cancer: 5-year survival 40-60% for node-negative, 20-30% for node-positive. Adjuvant chemotherapy: gemcitabine or 5-FU based.
Biliary drainage (ERCP + metal stent). Palliative systemic therapy. Prognosis better than pancreatic cancer but median survival still <12 months.
